title: Open-Meteo marine returns nearest-coastal data for inland/sheltered spots — suppress by water_type
description: Validation (c1v.1) proved the marine API returns the nearest COASTAL grid cell even for inland coords: New Forest Water Park (inland lake) got phantom wave 0.62m + sea-temp 19.2C from open sea ~11mi away; Christchurch Harbour (flat/sheltered) got 0.64m from the open-sea grid, over-penalising SUP/swim/wake. has_marine cannot detect this. Fix: thread spot water_type into the provider/coordinator and null wave/swell/water-temp fields when water_type=inland; consider an attenuation factor for sheltered (harbour) spots. Strategic fix at provider level, not per-call site.

title: Expose per-spot now/week detail as HA entity attributes (LVGL panel transport)
description: The get_spot_detail SERVICE (shipped v0.1.3+) feeds the Lovelace card, but an ESPHome LVGL wall panel (Waveshare P4 86-panel, 720x720) binds to HA ENTITY STATE/ATTRIBUTES via the native homeassistant sensor/text_sensor platforms — it cannot cleanly call a response-returning service and parse nested JSON. So the LVGL Swelligence screen (tracked in the HomeAutomation repo, epic HomeAutomation-4uq) is BLOCKED on exposing the per-spot now/week data as bindable HA entities. Options: (a) add attributes to the existing per-(spot,sport) suitability sensor for the near-term hourly series + best + tide phase (but tide_state/now-conditions are spot-level, not sport-level); (b) add a per-spot 'conditions' sensor whose attributes carry now (wind/gust/wave/swell/tide state+next), the 24h suitability series per sport, and the daytime weekly daily-peak rows (with peak-hour conditions+tide) — closest match to get_spot_detail, one entity per spot the panel binds to; (c) a compact text_sensor JSON the panel parses. Recommend (b): a SpotConditionsSensor per spot exposing tide_state + per-sport series/daily as attributes, reusing the exact builders get_spot_detail already calls (tide_state, tide_phase, build_forecast hourly pad_h=999 horizon=24, daily pad_h=0). Keep attribute payload within HA's recorder/size limits (exclude from recorder if large; the panel reads live state, not history). Honesty semantics unchanged (modelled vs overlay tide, daytime-only weekly). Map PNG for the panel is a SEPARATE concern (HA camera/script renders a static per-spot map image).
notes: Spawned 2026-06-25 from the HomeAutomation LVGL panel spec (esphome/design/swelligence-panel-spec.md, epic HomeAutomation-4uq). c1v.23 shipped the SERVICE transport (card); this is the ENTITY/state transport (LVGL panel). The two repos line up here.

title: Bundle Lovelace card with the integration (auto-register frontend resource, self-versioning)
description: The card (www/swelligence-card.js) is delivered manually (copied to HA www/ + a manual /local/...?v= Lovelace resource the user must cache-bust by hand). HACS updates the integration but not the card, so users miss card updates. Option A: ship the card INSIDE the integration package and auto-register it as a frontend module on setup. Move card to custom_components/swelligence/frontend/swelligence-card.js (HACS integration install ships it); in async_setup_entry (guarded once) register a static path + add_extra_js_url(hass, f'{url}?v={manifest version}') so the card auto-loads on every dashboard and the ?v= busts cache each release. Guard the card's customElements.define against double-define. One HACS install, self-versioning, no manual resource. User migration: remove the old /local/ resource. Update deploy docs.

title: Expose tide_state + daytime daily outlook as live data points (entity attrs / get_forecast) for card+panel
description: Thin-screen architecture (see bd memory thin-screen-integration-provides-datapoints): the HA card and LVGL panel must render integration-provided data points, not compute. The pure builders now exist — forecast.hourly_forecast (daylight hourly), forecast.daily_forecast (daytime-only daily PEAK = weekly outlook; use pad_h=0 for a true sunrise..sunset window, the default pad_h=2 barely filters a UK summer), tide.tide_state (rising/falling + next high/low; prefers real tide_events overlay, falls back to modelled sea_level_m). They are exercised by the mockup via scripts/sample_spot.py. PRODUCTIONIZE: surface them on the live integration so real consumers get them with zero screen-side processing — (a) tide_state as a per-spot sensor attribute (or its own sensor) refreshed each coordinator cycle; (b) the daytime daily outlook + near-term hourly series via the existing swelligence.get_forecast service response (and/or a compact attribute for the panel which can't call services easily). Decide the LVGL transport: the panel likely reads HA REST/websocket state, so the data must live in entity STATE/attributes, not only a service. Add tests + update docs/data-model.md. Note: tide_state currently labels 'modelled' for the sample (no overlay fetched); live spots with a UKHO/NOAA overlay will read 'overlay'.

title: 720x720 LVGL touch wall-panel + frontend-design refresh of spot-detail card
description: Two deliverables. (1) Frontend-design critique + targeted UI/UX refinement of the HA Lovelace spot-detail card (mockups/spot-detail.html) — keep it RESTRAINED and HA-theme-inheriting so it blends into any dashboard; improve typography (Montserrat numerals), hierarchy, touch targets, vane legibility. (2) A NEW standalone 720x720 wall-panel mockup (mockups/wall-panel-720.html) for an LVGL touch display. CRITICAL: LVGL is a native embedded widget toolkit — it CANNOT render HTML/CSS/SVG/web-fonts/slippy-maps. The HTML mock is a VISUAL SPEC built only from LVGL-translatable primitives (arc, bar, label, image, flex/grid containers, button press-states) + a widget mapping. Aesthetic: bold 'marine instrument cluster' kiosk (dark navy base, sea-glass teal accent, amber caution), information-dense in 720x720 without overload, touch-first (no hover; >=64px targets; visible press/selected states). Font Montserrat (distinctive AND LVGL-bundled). Map -> pre-rendered static PNG refreshed periodically on LVGL (Leaflet used in the HTML mock for fidelity). Round-display-safe: critical info within a centre circle, only chrome in corners. Shares real 3-spot DATA via scripts/build_card_data.py (extended to inject both files).
design: Dashboard card and wall panel intentionally diverge: card = HA-themed/restrained (inherits theme vars, blends in); panel = branded marine-instrument kiosk (own palette, bolder). LVGL widget mapping: score rings -> lv_arc; factor bars -> lv_bar; compass/wind -> lv_arc + canvas line (or baked image); map -> lv_img (static PNG); selectors -> lv_btn with checked state + lv_flex/lv_grid layout; text -> lv_label in compiled Montserrat sizes. No CSS hover, no animation reliance, no transparency-heavy effects LVGL can't cheaply do. Onshore/offshore label is DEFERRED to swelligence-slh.6 (leave a slot, do not fabricate).
notes: Spawned 2026-06-25 from the spot-detail map-hero thread (swelligence-4ds). Uses frontend-design skill. Onshore/offshore safety read is parked in swelligence-slh.6.

title: AI-inferred shore orientation + onshore/offshore classification + local-knowledge mining
description: The spot-detail card needs to answer 'is it safe to go?', which hinges on whether the wind is ONSHORE/OFFSHORE/CROSS-SHORE relative to THIS beach — not an abstract flow arrow. That requires a per-spot seaward/shore bearing. Manual entry per spot does not scale (user cannot give directions for every location). Two inference sources: (1) auto-derive each spot's seaward bearing from coastline geometry at its coords (e.g. OSM coastline / nearest land-sea boundary) so onshore/offshore can be computed with zero config; (2) tap local knowledge on the internet — spot guides written by locals routinely state which wind + swell directions work for which sports at a given break/beach — mine these to enrich per-spot direction windows (wind_dirs/swell_dirs) and the safety semantics. Classification: compare wind FROM-bearing to seaward bearing → onshore (from sea), offshore (from land), cross-shore. CRITICAL honesty (per safety-marker-design-principles + slh.2): onshore/offshore is a FACT about the coast; safe-vs-unsafe is SPORT-DEPENDENT — offshore is desirable for surf (grooms wave faces) but dangerous for SUP/sea-swim/beginner-wing/inflatables (blows you out to sea). So the classification feeds sport-aware safety flags (slh.2), it does not blanket-label offshore as unsafe. Worked example: Southbourne faces ~S (sea ~180deg); wind from NNW (336deg) blows from land out to sea = OFFSHORE.
design: Layering: shore-bearing source (auto-inferred, overridable) -> onshore/offshore/cross-shore classifier (pure) -> per-sport safety flags (slh.2) -> card UI label + vane colour (consumes classification). Inference must be cache/store-backed (don't hit the web per poll) and overridable by slh.3 manual config. Local-knowledge mining is a separate ingest step producing per-spot direction metadata + provenance; keep model output advisory and labelled (never present mined claims as certainty). Card consumer (swelligence-4ds map hero) already draws the wind vane in flow direction, so it only needs the classification + a coloured label band; the arrow itself is unchanged.
notes: Spawned from spot-detail map-hero card work (swelligence-4ds) on 2026-06-25. Relationship: slh.3 = manual per-spot safety metadata in config flow (the override/entry path); THIS bead = the auto-inference + web-knowledge source that makes it scale. Decided to defer building until card visual work (4ds) is finished.

title: Map-picker add-spot + postcode/area search (fix 'unknown' geocoding)
description: Open-Meteo geocoding can't resolve UK postcodes (BH23 4AA->0), outcodes (BH6->0), beach/spot names (Avon Beach->0), or qualified queries (Christchurch Dorset->0) — so search-first add-spot fails ('unknown') for real spots. Fix: (1) add postcodes.io (keyless UK) for postcode/outcode lookup, keep Open-Meteo for place names — unified async_geocode dispatcher. (2) Add a HA LocationSelector map step: search merely centres the map; user drags the pin to the exact point and commits. Every add goes through the map (visual confirmation -> no more bad coords). Flow: add_spot (search optional + name + water/sports/tide) -> pick (if multi-match, choose centre) -> add_spot_location (map) -> commit. Update strings + tests. Release as 0.1.2.
close_reason: Map picker + postcode/area search merged via PR #23 (CI green incl. HA guard map-flow tests). Releasing 0.1.2.

title: Release notes body from CHANGELOG (so HACS shows a summary, not just a link)
description: release.yml uses generate_release_notes:true, which derives notes from GitHub PRs — but PRs are merged on Forgejo and only mirrored to GitHub, so GitHub has no PR data and emits only a 'Full Changelog' compare link. HACS displays that body verbatim => users see just a link, no summary. Fix: (1) release.sh filters CHANGELOG noise (drop merge/chore(beads)/chore(release)); (2) release.yml extracts the current tag's CHANGELOG.md section + appends a compare link and publishes it as the release body (body_path), dropping generate_release_notes; checkout needs fetch-depth:0 for the prev-tag. Backfill v0.1.1/v0.1.0 bodies via API so the current HACS view is fixed too.
close_reason: release.yml publishes CHANGELOG section as body; release.sh filters noise + fixes header placement; CHANGELOG repaired; v0.1.1/v0.1.0 bodies backfilled (HTTP 200). Merged PR #22.

title: Search-first add-spot config flow (place-name geocoding primary, coords as fallback)

Redesign add_spot to be search-first: step 1 captures water/sports/tide + an optional custom-name override + a place-search query (or a 'enter coordinates manually' toggle for unnamed breaks); the geocode result supplies name+coords; multi-match -> pick step; manual toggle -> add_spot_coords step (name+lat+lon). Name now derives from the matched place unless overridden. Update strings.json + translations/en.json (+ geocode_query_required error) and README. Release as 0.1.1.

title: release.sh triggers Forgejo push-mirror sync on release (sync-on-release model)
description: User wants the GitHub mirror to propagate only at release time, not per-commit (sync_on_commit stays false). Add a sync-trigger to scripts/release.sh: after pushing the tag to Forgejo origin, POST /repos/{owner}/{repo}/push_mirrors-sync (owner/repo derived from origin URL, token from git credential) so the tag reaches GitHub immediately and release.yml fires. Best-effort (warn, don't fail the release if the trigger errors; the 8h interval + manual 'Synchronize Now' are backstops). Add --no-mirror-sync opt-out. Update docs/releasing.md to reflect sync-on-release (don't tick 'sync on commit').

title: HACS release pipeline: GitHub mirror + tag-triggered versioned releases
description: HACS is GitHub-only and the repo is Forgejo-only with no tags/releases, so it cannot currently be installed via HACS. Establish: Forgejo stays primary with a one-way push-mirror to github.com/f00lycooly/swelligence (the HACS-facing release surface); a tag-triggered GitHub Action that validates (tests/guard/hassfest/hacs-action), asserts manifest.version==tag, and publishes a Release; a local conventional-commit version-bump helper. Chosen design: Option 2 (Forgejo primary + tag Action), not release-please, to honour the global Forgejo-primary rule.
close_reason: HACS release pipeline complete: GitHub mirror (sync-on-release), tag-triggered release.yml + bump helper, hacs.json/hacs-action, v0.1.0 published, HACS install verified at v0.1.0.

title: Safety flags as first-class output (separate from score)
description: Codex safety-marker proposal. Add safety_flags as a first-class scored-result field + entity attribute, SEPARATE from score/confidence (score=how good, confidence=how trustworthy, safety_flags=why it may be unsafe). Derive conservatively from data: offshore-wind risk (positive for surf, NEGATIVE for SUP/sea-swim/beginner-wing/inflatables; needs spot wind-direction metadata + the offshore-unsafe toggle), strong-gust / gust-spread, and expose the existing wind/wave HARD-FAILS as explicit flags (too strong / too big / too choppy) — unify rather than parallel-mechanism. Cold-water (esp. sea swim). Current risk from current_speed_kn/dir as a MODEL HINT only. Elevated-rip-risk: NOT directly observed — infer from surf size + swell period + tide + current + wind/wave, and label 'elevated rip risk', NEVER 'rip present'. Selected flags may optionally cap score, but only where sport-relevant. HONESTY + RESTRAINT are acceptance criteria: conservative thresholds, individually-justified flags, no over-flagging. Depends on completeness semantics + the new scored fields (48w.7).
acceptance_criteria: safety_flags exposed separately from score; flags derive from data with conservative thresholds; inferred risks labeled as elevated/possible not certain; currents framed as model hints; selected flags cap score only per sport-relevance; over-flagging guarded (tests assert calm/benign conditions produce no flags); unit tests per flag.

title: Factor completeness semantics + essential-missing caps
description: FOUNDATION (Codex #1/#2). score_point currently drops None factors from the weighted-mean denominator, treating 'unknown' as 'doesn't matter' — which inflates scores when essential data is missing and lets untuned spots (no wind_dirs/swell_dirs) escape strictness a tuned spot gets. Distinguish three states per factor: not_applicable (sport genuinely doesn't score it — stays free), not_configured (spot metadata missing, e.g. no offshore window — surface as a data-quality nudge, don't silently relax), missing_provider_data (provider returned None for a field the sport DOES score — cap/penalize). Add a per-sport set of ESSENTIAL factors; when an essential factor is missing, cap the score (or apply a completeness penalty) rather than averaging it away. Examples: surf missing swell quality, swim missing water temp, wind sport missing direction on a spot that has an offshore window. Keep raw score reflecting conditions. This blocks the safety-flag + recalibration work.
acceptance_criteria: Factor results carry a completeness state (n/a vs not_configured vs missing_data); essential-missing per sport caps/penalizes the score with a reason; not_configured surfaces as a nudge; existing tests updated; new tests cover each state.
notes: Implemented on feature/slh-1-factor-completeness, PR #17. Four completeness states (applicable/not_applicable/not_configured/missing_data); essential-missing caps at INCOMPLETE_CAP=50; conservative per-sport essential_factors (wind everywhere, marine only for surf/seaswim/sea-wakeboard). ScoreResult.completeness + nudges exposed on sensor. Scores unchanged when data present -> no recalibration. 6 new tests. Awaiting CI+merge before close.
close_reason: Implemented + merged via PR #17. Factor completeness states + essential-missing cap (50). Foundation for slh.2.

title: Scoring fairness + safety markers
description: From a Codex scoring review (2026-06-24) of docs/scoring.md vs scoring.py. The deterministic scorer is directionally sound (transparent, sport-profiled, hard-fail caps, tide/kit gates, ranking separate from raw score) but has fairness gaps and lacks a safety dimension. (a) Too optimistic when essential data is missing: score_point drops None factors from the denominator, so surf with no swell period, or swim with no water temp, scores as if the unknown doesn't matter. (b) Untuned spots are advantaged: direction only scores when wind_dirs/swell_dirs are configured, so a tuned spot is judged more harshly than an unset one. (c) Hard-fails are wind/wave-only and generic. (d) Tide gate is soft (floor 0.3, reached only after >2 windows) — weak for tide-critical/inaccessible launches. (e) Calibration is narrow (Christchurch/Open-Meteo only). Structural proposal: SAFETY is a separate first-class marker, not another weight: score=how good, confidence=how trustworthy, safety_flags=why it may be unsafe; flags may optionally cap score per sport. Honesty rule: conservative, inferred labels ('elevated rip risk', never 'rip present'; currents are model hints, not beach certainty). Caution: over-flagging destroys trust — flags must be sparing and individually justified.
design: Foundation = factor-completeness semantics: distinguish not_applicable (sport doesn't score it), not_configured (spot metadata missing), missing_provider_data (provider None). Essential-but-missing fields cap/flag per sport instead of dropping silently; not_configured surfaces as a nudge (don't silently relax strictness). Unify the existing wind/wave hard-fails INTO the safety-flag model (too strong/too big become explicit flags) rather than a parallel mechanism. Safety flags derive conservatively from existing+new fields. Per-spot safety metadata extends the config flow. Recalibrate against a broad fixture matrix LAST, after scoring changes land. Keep raw score reflecting conditions; sport priority stays ranking-only.
acceptance_criteria: Essential-missing fields no longer silently inflate scores; not_configured vs missing_data vs n/a are distinguished and surfaced; safety_flags is a first-class output separate from score with conservative honest labels; selected flags cap score only where sport-relevant; per-spot tide/offshore safety metadata exists; profiles recalibrated against a broadened fixture matrix; tests green.

title: Wire new Open-Meteo detail fields into scoring + recalibrate profiles
description: unr captured 12 additional-detail fields (swell_peak_period_s, wind_wave_*, secondary_swell_*, current_*, sea_level_m, apparent_temp_c, uv_index, visibility_m, weather_code) into ForecastPoint + the get_forecast service, but did NOT change scoring (deliberately, to avoid destabilising the calibrated sport profiles). This bead consumes them: (1) surf quality — use swell_peak_period_s as the surf-power proxy and the wind_wave vs swell split for clean-vs-messy; secondary_swell for confused-sea penalty; (2) safety — optional current-speed flag for SUP/foil/kite/swim; (3) tide — optionally use continuous sea_level_m alongside discrete events. MANDATORY: re-run validate_spots.py + analyze_history.py after any profile/weight change per the sport-profiles calibration memory, and update confidence FIELD_UNCERTAINTY_SCALE/_FACTOR_FIELDS for any newly-scored field so 48w.1 confidence aggregates them. Capture + service exposure already done (committed).
acceptance_criteria: Selected new fields feed scoring with documented rationale; sport profiles re-calibrated (both scripts re-run, results recorded); FIELD_UNCERTAINTY_SCALE covers newly-scored fields; tests green.

title: Remove Windy provider, config-flow option, and tests
description: Delete the Windy provider entirely. Windy is paid-key only, forbids data redistribution, and adds no data Open-Meteo lacks (it serves the same GFS/gfsWave fields). Remove: providers/windy.py; its registration; Windy config-flow/options entries; related strings/translations; and its unit tests. Update CLAUDE.md/README/research notes that reference Windy as a live provider. Independent of the tide work (Windy provided no tides), so no blockers — can land any time in the epic.
acceptance_criteria: No windy code/config/strings/tests remain; integration loads and HA guard passes without Windy; docs updated.
close_reason: Windy provider removed: providers/windy.py deleted; dropped from PROVIDERS registry + __all__; config-flow docstring, strings/translations key, authority ranking, and all tests (test_domains, test_providers_keyed, test_authority, test_overlay, HA guard) updated; README/SCOPE docs updated. pytest green (163 passed).

title: Remove Stormglass provider, config-flow option, and free-tier cap code
description: Delete the Stormglass provider entirely now that (a) Open-Meteo is the sole forecast source and provides the same underlying wave models, and (b) tides come from the region-resolving TideProvider (UKHO/CO-OPS/Open-Meteo fallback). Remove: providers/stormglass.py; its registration; the Stormglass config-flow/options entries; the free_tier / free_tier_min_interval_minutes() machinery that existed to ration Stormglass's 10 req/day budget (verify nothing else depends on it); related strings/translations; and its unit tests. Update CLAUDE.md/README/research notes that reference Stormglass as a live provider. BLOCKED BY the confidence re-source and the tide resolver so neither regresses on removal.
acceptance_criteria: No stormglass code/config/strings/tests remain; integration loads and HA guard passes without Stormglass; confidence and tides still function; docs updated.
close_reason: Stormglass provider removed: providers/stormglass.py deleted; dropped from PROVIDERS + TIDE_PROVIDERS + __all__; config-flow key field + free-tier toggle auto-drop (registry-derived) + providers-step guard fixed for UKHO; strings/translations cleaned; ~9 test files updated (test_authority rewritten around TIDE hierarchy, test_free_tier uses synthetic provider, Stormglass-specific cases removed); docstrings + README/SCOPE updated. Dependency on 48w.1 dropped per decision (confidence degrades gracefully to absent until re-sourced). Generic marine-overlay/ensemble + free-tier machinery left dormant for 48w.6. Tide stack now UKHO/CO-OPS/Open-Meteo-modeled. pytest 166 passed.

title: Enabling refactor: declarative overlay-capability model + generic region/priority resolver
description: Turn provider integration into a wiring point, not gut-and-replace. Today provider identity leaks into orchestration: TideProvider has no region/priority (base.py), so authority.py hardcodes _UK_BBOX/_uk_tide for UKHO; coordinator.py has two bespoke overlay methods (_apply_marine_overlay, _apply_tide); authority.py keeps a central DOMAIN_AUTHORITY table edited per provider. Abstraction: (1) add covers(lat,lon)->bool + priority:int to the overlay/TideProvider ABC (default covers=global, priority=0); (2) a pure resolve_overlay(domain,lat,lon,available)->key = highest-priority registered overlay whose covers() is true and whose key requirement is met; (3) collapse the two coordinator methods into one generic overlay loop driven by declared capability + a merge-mode per overlay (tide=event-augment, marine=field gap-fill/prefer/blend via existing overlay.py); (4) authority.py 'better source' nudge derives from the SAME provider-declared metadata instead of DOMAIN_AUTHORITY. Open-Meteo modeled sea-level fallback is just the priority-0 global tide entry (derive high/low from sea_level_height_msl, label 'indicative'). RESULT: removing Stormglass = delete file + unregister; adding UKHO/CO-OPS/WorldTides = leaf class declaring covers()/priority + register; neither touches coordinator/authority. This unblocks parallel, non-conflicting fan-out of 48w.3 + 7sw + UKHO-wire + 48w.5.
design: Keep what's already declarative (registries, ForecastProvider capability attrs, source_confidence, stamp_sources, overlay.py merge mechanics). Net new: covers()/priority on the overlay ABC; pure resolver (testable, no I/O); generic coordinator overlay loop; metadata-driven authority. Merge-mode distinction matters: tide overlays augment SpotForecast.tide_events; marine/swell overlays gap-fill/replace ForecastPoint fields — encode as an overlay attribute so one loop handles both.
acceptance_criteria: covers()/priority on the overlay ABC; pure resolver picks correct provider by region+priority+key-availability with global fallback; coordinator applies overlays via one generic loop (tide events + marine fields both work); authority nudges derive from provider metadata (DOMAIN_AUTHORITY table gone or generated); Open-Meteo sea-level fallback registered as priority-0 global tide; existing tests pass; new resolver unit tests; no provider key strings hardcoded in coordinator/authority.
notes:
HUMAN DECISION (screen finalisation): Is Open-Meteo's MODELED sea-level (derived high/low) good enough as the GLOBAL default tide for non-UK/US spots? Live test showed it carries a tidal signal but is noisy at hard locations (Solent double tides). Options: (a) accept modeled fallback as zero-config default labelled 'indicative'; (b) require a WorldTides key outside UK/US. Recommend validating derived-tide quality on a normal open-coast spot before accepting.
PROGRESS — DONE: covers()/authority_rank ABCs, metadata-derived authority, resolve_overlay/domain_ranking, coordinator tide auto-resolution, assert_legal_domains gate at registry + authority maps (commit d10de14). REMAINING: (1) collapse coordinator _apply_marine_overlay + _apply_tide into one generic overlay loop and route marine through resolve_overlay so swell authorities are also pure wiring points; (2) Open-Meteo sea-level priority-0 global tide fallback (leaf).
close_reason: Enabling refactor delivered (commits d10de14, +fallback): covers()/authority_rank on the overlay ABCs; metadata-derived authority (hardcoded DOMAIN_AUTHORITY table + _uk_tide/_sea_only gone); pure resolve_overlay/domain_ranking; coordinator auto-resolves tide source by region/priority; assert_legal_domains legality gate at the registry + authority maps; Open-Meteo modeled tide registered as the keyless priority-0 global fallback. Provider integration is now a wiring point: removing Stormglass = unregister; adding UKHO/CO-OPS/WorldTides = leaf class + register. pytest 172 passed. DEFERRED to 48w.6: collapsing the marine overlay/ensemble machinery into the generic loop (it's dead once Stormglass is removed).

title: Re-source confidence from Open-Meteo models= multi-model
description: Today forecast confidence (o07) comes from cross-provider/intra-Stormglass model spread via confidence.field_confidence (needs >=2 sources). Removing Stormglass kills that signal and aggregate_confidence returns None — confidence goes dark for a single provider. Fix at the abstraction level: confidence.py is already provider-agnostic, so make the Open-Meteo provider request the SAME variables from multiple models via the keyless models= parameter (e.g. gfs_wave + ecmwf_wam + meteofrance_wam for waves; gfs_seamless/ecmwf_ifs/etc. for wind), then populate ForecastPoint.source_confidence from the per-model spread exactly as Stormglass did from its {sg,noaa,icon,...} dict. Result: cross-MODEL agreement replaces cross-PROVIDER agreement, keeping the o07 confidence/label story alive with one keyless provider. MUST land BEFORE Stormglass removal so confidence never regresses. Verify models= returns per-model arrays in one call (1 extra call cost, no key).
acceptance_criteria: Open-Meteo populates source_confidence from multi-model spread; aggregate_confidence returns a value+label for an Open-Meteo-only spot; confidence unit tests updated to the multi-model path; no dependency on any keyed provider for confidence.
notes: ORDERING (decided 2026-06-24): blocked by swelligence-unr (field set) AND swelligence-akc (batched/multi-call request shape). Confidence aggregates per-FIELD model agreement (FIELD_UNCERTAINTY_SCALE / _FACTOR_FIELDS in confidence.py), so the complete fetched field set and the final Open-Meteo request shape must be settled first — otherwise every field added later forces a confidence revisit. When implementing, add the models= multi-model dimension on top of the already-extended field list + batched request, and ensure FIELD_UNCERTAINTY_SCALE covers any new scored fields (e.g. swell peak period, wind-wave).

title: Single-source simplification: Open-Meteo forecast + regional-authority tides
description: Reverse the multi-provider layering (epic al8) and provider-authority work (o07.4) in favour of Open-Meteo as the SOLE forecast source, with confidence re-sourced from Open-Meteo's models= multi-model, and tides from regional authorities behind a region-resolving TideProvider (UKHO=UK, NOAA CO-OPS=US, optional WorldTides global) with a keyless Open-Meteo modeled sea-level fallback everywhere else. Drops Stormglass and Windy entirely. Rationale (see mockups/research/): paid aggregators (Stormglass/Windy) repackage the SAME public wave models (NOAA GFS-Wave, MFWAM, ECMWF-WAM) Open-Meteo serves keyless, so they add no forecast accuracy; Stormglass free tier (10 req/day) is unusable for polling and can't batch coordinates; Windy is paid-key only and forbids redistribution. For the at-a-glance in-home 'is it good enough + next few days' use case, Open-Meteo is sufficient. This epic also obsoletes the o07.4 provider-authority map + 'better source' nudges (no alternative source to nudge toward) — the 'data sources' UI collapses to single-source + a multi-model confidence/agreement badge, which has screen-design implications for the in-progress mockups.
design: Strategic, abstraction-level change. (1) Confidence decouples from cross-PROVIDER spread to cross-MODEL spread via Open-Meteo models= — confidence.py is already provider-agnostic, so only the open_meteo provider needs to fetch multiple models and populate source_confidence. (2) Tides move behind a region-resolving TideProvider (same TideProvider abstraction UKHO already implements): resolve by coordinate -> UKHO (UK), NOAA CO-OPS (US), WorldTides (keyed global, optional), else Open-Meteo sea_level_height_msl modeled fallback (zero-config, indicative). (3) Stormglass + Windy providers, their config-flow options, and Stormglass free-tier cap code are removed. Order matters: stand up the confidence re-source and tide resolver BEFORE removing Stormglass so neither confidence nor tide coverage regresses.
acceptance_criteria: Open-Meteo is the only forecast provider; Stormglass and Windy code/config/tests removed; confidence still produced from Open-Meteo multi-model; every spot gets tides (authority-grade in UK/US, indicative elsewhere) with zero required keys; source/authority UI reflects single-source + confidence; pytest + HA guard green; sport profiles re-calibrated where scoring inputs changed.

title: Add NOAA CO-OPS tide provider (US, free/no-key, harmonic)
description: Add NOAA CO-OPS (Tides & Currents) as the US tide provider behind the region-resolver — the direct US equivalent of UKHO. Live-confirmed: free, NO API key, harmonic predictions, JSON, US public domain (no per-user key, no redistribution limit). Same pattern as ukho.py: resolve nearest station via the Metadata API (https://api.tidesandcurrents.noaa.gov/mdapi/prod/), then fetch high/low events via the Data API (https://api.tidesandcurrents.noaa.gov/api/prod/?product=predictions&interval=hilo&datum=MLLW&format=json&time_zone=gmt) and normalise to TideEvent. Bonus free fields available from CO-OPS: currents + water temp (optional, later). Gate activation to US coordinates in the resolver. See mockups/research/.
acceptance_criteria: A US coordinate resolves to CO-OPS; nearest-station lookup + hilo predictions normalise to TideEvent; no API key required; region gate prevents non-US use; unit tests cover station resolution + event parsing.
close_reason: NOAA CO-OPS tide provider added as a leaf (providers/noaa_coops.py): keyless, US-region-gated covers(), authority_rank={TIDE:100}, nearest-station + hilo predictions -> TideEvent. Auto-resolves for US coords; surfaces in UI from registry; no config-flow/coordinator/authority edits needed (wiring point confirmed). Tests: nearest-station, prediction parsing, US covers() gate, US resolver win. pytest 176 passed.

title: Extend Open-Meteo variable sets with 8 additional-detail marine fields
description: Add richer marine/forecast fields that ride free on the existing keyless Open-Meteo calls (just extend _FORECAST_HOURLY / _MARINE_HOURLY): swell_wave_peak_period (surf-power proxy), wind_wave_height+wind_wave_period (clean-vs-messy split), secondary_swell_wave_* (crossed-swell detection), ocean_current_velocity+direction (rip/drift safety), sea_level_height_msl (fills the currently-empty ForecastPoint.sea_level_m for continuous tide scoring), apparent_temperature (wetsuit comfort), visibility+uv_index (offshore safety/exposure), weather_code (human-readable card label). Wire the swell/wind-wave fields into scoring and sea_level into tide. IMPORTANT: re-calibrate sport profiles after scoring changes per the 'sport-profiles-in-sports-py-were-calibrated-against' memory (re-run validate_spots.py + analyze_history.py). See mockups/research/api-field-mapping.md.
acceptance_criteria: New fields fetched and normalised into the domain model; swell_wave_peak_period/wind_wave_*/secondary_swell_* feed scoring; sea_level_height_msl populates sea_level_m and tide; sport profiles re-calibrated and tests green.
notes: DO FIRST (before confidence 48w.1). This is the authoritative 'capture everything' field pass: lock in the COMPLETE set of Open-Meteo variables we fetch (the 8 additional-detail fields + anything else worth capturing) so confidence and scoring build on the final shape. Coordinate with swelligence-akc — both rewrite the open_meteo request builder; do them together or land unr first to avoid merge churn. After this + akc, confidence (48w.1) layers models= on the settled request.
close_reason: Data-capture + exposure delivered: 12 additional-detail fields (swell_peak_period_s, wind_wave_height/period, secondary_swell_*, current_speed_kn/dir, sea_level_m, apparent_temp_c, uv_index, visibility_m, weather_code) fetched on the existing 2 Open-Meteo calls (variable names+units live-validated), normalised into ForecastPoint, grouped in DOMAIN_FIELDS, and surfaced via get_forecast. sea_level_m now populated (was declared-but-empty). Scoring consumption + profile recalibration deliberately split into a follow-up (riskier, needs validate_spots.py + analyze_history.py re-run) per the 'capture everything first' plan. pytest 176 passed.

title: Open-Meteo request batching: collapse N spots to 2 batched calls
description: Open-Meteo supports comma-separated lat/lon lists returning a per-location JSON array on BOTH the forecast and marine endpoints (live-confirmed 2026-06-24, tested up to 200 coords). Today the provider makes 2 HTTP calls PER spot; batching collapses all N spots to 2 calls total (1 forecast + 1 marine), a large quota/latency win. Implementation needs two parser changes: (1) single location returns a top-level object, multiple returns a top-level array, so branch on isinstance(payload, list) — current _merge/_parse_sun assume dict; (2) match results by ARRAY INDEX not coordinate equality, since snapped grid coords differ from inputs and differ between the forecast and marine grids. Open-Meteo-specific (Stormglass/Windy/UKHO stay one-call-per-spot). See mockups/research/open-meteo-findings.md.
acceptance_criteria: Provider issues 2 batched Open-Meteo calls regardless of spot count; results correctly index-matched per spot; existing tests pass; new test covers object-vs-array branch and index matching.
notes: DO FIRST (before confidence 48w.1). The batched request shape (N coords -> 2 calls, object-vs-array + index matching) must be settled before confidence adds the models= multi-model dimension on top. Coordinate with swelligence-unr (both touch the open_meteo request builder).
close_reason: Open-Meteo batching delivered: async_fetch_many issues 2 batched calls (1 forecast + 1 marine) for all coords regardless of spot count, handles single-coord object vs multi-coord array, index-matches results, per-coord marine flag (inland marine-free, marine call skipped if none want it). OpenMeteoBatchLoader (TTL+lock) shares one batched fetch across all per-spot coordinators; wired in setup. Tests cover object-vs-array, index matching, per-coord marine, all-inland skip, empty, and loader caching/coalescing. pytest 176 passed.

title: Confidence from Stormglass multi-source spread (free, intra-provider)
description: Stormglass returns several source models per field ({sg, noaa, icon, ...}); _pick() keeps 'sg' and DISCARDS the rest. That discarded spread is a free confidence signal. Capture per-field model agreement (e.g. std/range across sources) and derive a 0..1 confidence. No extra API calls. Surface a 'confidence' attribute on sensors + in get_overview.
design: Extend StormglassProvider parsing to retain the multi-source spread for the params we score; map spread -> confidence (tight spread=high). Confidence rides alongside the score, not folded into it.
acceptance_criteria: Stormglass-backed sensors show a confidence value derived from inter-model spread; high disagreement -> low confidence; zero extra requests.
close_reason: Confidence from Stormglass inter-model spread shipped: field_confidence + aggregate_confidence (confidence.py), _spread() retains discarded {noaa,icon,...} values, surfaced as confidence/confidence_label on sensors and get_overview. Zero extra API calls.

title: Surface data availability/quality per sensor (data_quality attribute)
description: We already compute availability/resolution facts in source_meta (marine unavailable, windsea-only swell, model name) but don't expose them usefully. Add a per-sensor data_quality attribute summarising what's thin or missing for that (spot,sport), and capture nearest-grid-cell distance where a provider returns it (a coarse cell far offshore is a quality signal). Cheap enabler for the rest of the epic; builds on al8.1 provenance.
acceptance_criteria: Each suitability sensor exposes a data_quality attribute (e.g. 'swell: open_meteo, windsea-only, no groundswell direction'); grid distance surfaced where available.
close_reason: data_quality attribute shipped: per-sport quality summary (quality.py), grid-cell distance captured in open_meteo, haversine deduped into geo.py

title: Forecast confidence & source intelligence (data quality, model agreement, better-source nudges)
description: Answer the question the suitability score can't: 'is this forecast good enough, and is there a better source?'. Today the integration surfaces provenance (data_sources, al8.1) + the score, but no measure of CONFIDENCE or data quality, and no comparison across providers. This epic adds the intelligence layer: (1) surface data availability/quality per sensor; (2) derive confidence from model agreement (spread) — starting with the Stormglass multi-source values we currently discard, then cross-provider; (3) a per-domain provider-authority map that proactively flags when a better source is available for a spot; (4) feed spread+sources to the LLM so the semantic verdict expresses confidence in words.
design: Confidence == agreement. Tight agreement between independent models -> trust; wide divergence -> low confidence/hedge. The cross-provider fetch reuses the al8.2 overlay machinery + free-tier throttle. Surfaces: sensor attributes (confidence, data_quality), get_overview (confidence per entry), HA Repairs/diagnostic (better-source nudges), and the AI Task verdict (confidence in plain language). Builds entirely on al8.1 per-field provenance. Reframes al8.5 (ensemble-for-accuracy) as confidence-from-spread; one multi-source fetch yields both the spread (confidence) and an optional consensus blend (accuracy).
acceptance_criteria: A sensor shows a confidence signal + a data-quality note alongside its score; low cross-provider agreement reads as low confidence; the system proactively flags a spot using a weaker-than-available source for a domain; the LLM verdict references model agreement.
close_reason: All 5 children complete. Forecast confidence & source intelligence layer shipped: per-sensor data_quality (o07.1), intra-provider Stormglass confidence (o07.2), cross-provider ensemble + blend (o07.3), provider-authority nudges (o07.4), LLM confidence verdict (o07.5). Built entirely on al8.1 provenance; supersedes al8.5.

title: Scoring consumes swell period/direction + offshore-direction (surf quality)
description: Better marine data only changes outcomes once the scorer reads it. Today surf is scored on wind strength alone (see calibration memory). Fold swell period/height/direction and offshore-wind-direction into the surf-family scoring so the layered data has effect. Overlaps with the known surf residual (M1/c1v.2) — reconcile with that rather than duplicate.
acceptance_criteria: Surf-type sports score using swell + offshore direction; a light offshore day no longer over/under-scored purely on wind; re-run validate_spots.py + analyze_history.py per calibration protocol.
close_reason: Surf scores swell quality (period x direction window) via _swell_factor; swell_dir_deg added + fetched by all providers; per-spot swell overrides. Re-calibrated, no regressions. Merged PR #8.

title: Budget-aware marine gap-fill / quality overlay (Stormglass marine onto Open-Meteo)
description: Keep Open-Meteo as the free wind base; when a sea spot lacks marine data (no marine grid) or better swell is wanted, overlay Stormglass marine (waves/swell/water-temp) onto the existing points by timestamp — the same overlay-merge mechanism M5 uses for tides. Fetched at the free-tier interval, NOT every coordinator tick, so it respects the 10/day budget. Coalesce semantics: overlay fills None / optionally replaces by configured priority.
design: Reuse free_tier_min_interval_minutes + the M5 coordinator overlay-attach wiring. Overlay producer returns marine ForecastPoint fields aligned by time; merge coalesces into the base SpotForecast.points. Cache overlay between base ticks to avoid per-tick paid calls.
acceptance_criteria: A sea spot with no Open-Meteo marine grid gains waves/swell from Stormglass without exceeding the free budget; wind still from Open-Meteo; source_meta reflects both.
close_reason: Marine gap-fill/prefer overlay (overlay.py merge_marine) wired in coordinator with TTL-cached shared overlay fetch (reused for tides), per-domain provenance re-stamp, free-tier throttle. Merged PR #7.

title: Per-field source provenance in source_meta (enabling)
description: Track which provider supplied each data domain so a merged SpotForecast is debuggable and explainable. Extend source_meta to record e.g. {wind: open_meteo, waves: stormglass, tide: ukho}. Surfaced on sensor attributes and fed to the LLM verdict. Enabling task for the composite merge.
acceptance_criteria: Merged forecasts carry per-domain source attribution; single-source forecasts still report their one source.
close_reason: Domain taxonomy (providers/domains.py) + per-domain source provenance stamped by each provider into source_meta['sources']; surfaced as sensor data_sources attr + in LLM payload. Merged PR #5.

title: Multi-source forecast layering (composite provider / per-domain source overlays)
description: Evolve from one-provider-per-spot (winner-takes-all) to a composite model where each data domain (wind, waves/swell, tide, temps) is sourced from the best available provider and merged field-by-field into a single normalised SpotForecast. Keeps keyless Open-Meteo as the always-on free base; keyed/paid sources (Stormglass, Windy, UKHO) become surgical, budget-aware quality overlays rather than wholesale swaps. The normalised SpotForecast shape, per-field None semantics, timestamp-aligned merges, and the existing TideProvider overlay already establish the foundation.
design: Strategic abstraction: a CompositeProvider that, per spot, maps {domain -> source} and coalesces by priority. Single-provider is the trivial config. Three layering modes: (A) per-domain routing, (B) coalesce/gap-fill, (C) ensemble/consensus. Cost is the dominant constraint — layering multiplies metered calls, so overlays reuse the free-tier throttle and the M5 overlay-attach wiring (fetched at overlay cadence, not every coordinator tick). Per-field provenance tracked in source_meta. Scoring must consume the richer fields for any of this to change outcomes.
acceptance_criteria: A sea spot with no Open-Meteo marine grid still gets waves/swell from a keyed overlay without exceeding free-tier budgets; source of each field is visible in source_meta; per-domain routing is configurable per spot; single-provider behaviour unchanged when no overlay configured.

title: Free-tier checkbox on providers screen auto-sets safe rate limit
description: On the providers API-key options step, add a 'Free tier' checkbox per keyed provider that has a known free quota (Stormglass: 10 req/day). When checked, the integration auto-derives a safe minimum poll interval from the provider's daily budget and requests-per-fetch, divided across the spots using that provider, so continuous polling can't exhaust the free quota. Provider declares free_tier_daily_requests + requests_per_fetch; coordinator interval = max(user interval, computed safe interval).
close_reason: Free-tier checkbox per keyed provider + auto-derived safe poll interval (free_tier_min_interval_minutes). Stormglass 10/day -> 6h/spot. Merged PR #4.

close_reason: Implemented edit_spot/edit_spot_fields options-flow steps in config_flow.py + strings; removing a sport prunes its per-spot prefs.

title: M9: expose forecast timeline (future suitability per spot x sport)
description:
Serve future suitability per (spot x sport) following HA weather best-practice (2024.4+): forecast is NOT entity state/attributes and NOT a per-day sensor explosion. Decisions (researched + confirmed):
- RESOLUTION: hourly. HORIZON: 7 days. WINDOW: sunrise-2h..sunset+2h (Open-Meteo daily sunrise/sunset).
- DELIVERY: a service swelligence.get_forecast (SupportsResponse.ONLY) mirroring weather.get_forecasts. Target suitability entity (or {spot,sport}); arg type: hourly|daily.
  * hourly entry: {datetime, score, verdict, suitable, wind_speed_kn, wind_gust_kn, wind_bearing, wave_height_m, water_temp_c, kit_ideal_m2, kit_rig_m2, kit_power}
  * daily entry: per-day BEST slot {date, datetime, score, verdict, suitable, kit_*}
  * kit recommendation computed PER timestep (wind varies).
- Keep existing now sensor/binary_sensor as state=NOW (+ existing best_in_24h). No forecast attributes.
- Coordinator: extend fetch 48h -> 7 days (wind + marine + daily sunrise/sunset); store points; score lazily for the service.
- M3 card (c1v.4) renders daily tiles + hourly drilldown by calling the service (like the weather forecast card).
- OPTIONAL/secondary: one real weather entity per spot (WeatherEntityFeature.FORECAST_HOURLY|DAILY + async_forecast_*) for raw conditions so native weather cards work.
Refs: developers.home-assistant.io/docs/core/entity/weather; forecast attribute removed in 2024.4.
notes: Implemented + verified live. 7-day fetch + sunrise/sunset; pure forecast.py (hourly/daily, sunrise-2..sunset+2 window, per-timestep kit); swelligence.get_forecast service (hourly|daily, SupportsResponse.ONLY) + services.yaml; sensors self-register as targets. No forecast-in-attributes (HA best practice). Live: daily 7d + hourly 147 slots, inland suppressed, no errors.

Add a guard: either pytest-homeassistant-custom-component import tests, or a CI step that pip-installs homeassistant and imports each platform module. Interim guard: the docker-exec import check documented in DEPLOY.md step 1.5.
notes: Now TWO real HA-import/schema bugs slipped past the stubbed suite: DeviceInfo wrong module (c1v.10-era), and NumberSelector step=0.0001 invalid. Strongly justifies a CI guard that imports modules AND renders/serialises config+options-flow schemas against real HA (pytest-homeassistant-custom-component).

title: Sizing model + calibration (sizing.py): weight/wind -> ideal kite/wing size

notes: Pure sizing.py + 15 tests. ideal_size (inverse weight/wind model) + recommend_kit (nearest-owned power match: ideal/under/over/no_kit/n-a). Constants calibratable. Unblocks c1v.12.

title: M8: rider personalisation — profile + quiver-aware kite/wing recommendations
description: Personalise powered-sport suitability to the single local rider. RiderProfile (weight kg, optional ability) + one Quiver (owned kite sizes m2, wing sizes m2, optional boards/foils) in config — no multi-user modelling, no per-rider entity multiplication. Calibratable sizing model (sizing.py): ideal_kite_m2 ~ C_kite*weight/wind_kn (C_kite~2.25), ideal_wing_m2 ~ C_wing*weight/wind_kn (C_wing~1.0). Quiver-aware scoring: pick nearest owned size to ideal -> power-match factor (ideal=full, off-size=under/over penalty, nothing usable=cap low). Output per powered-sport sensor: recommended_size, owned_size_to_rig, power verdict. Feed rider context to the LLM verdict too. See docs/SCOPE.md Personalisation section.
notes: Quiver-aware scoring live: blend_kit folds power-match into kite/wing 'now' score (40% floor), recommendation on sensor attrs + LLM prompt, rider/quiver options step. Proven: epic with right kit, marginal on wrong/no kit. 53 tests pass.

title: Calibrate sport profiles: graduated wave factor + softer gust handling
description: From c1v.1 validation: (1) wave factor is binary within range so 0.6m reads 'epic' for surf — ramp toward an ideal wave height per sport instead of full credit anywhere in [min,max]. (2) Light-wind sports (SUP, sea swim, wakeboard) hard-fail to a 30 cap the instant a gust exceeds a low ceiling (16-18kn); consider a graduated gust penalty rather than the blunt 30-floor. Revisit gust_max + wave windows per profile.
notes: Graduated wave factor (wave_ideal_m + comfort plateau) and graduated gust penalty (dropped from hard-fail cap). Re-validated across all 6 spots: surf no longer epic at 0.6m, light-wind sports sensible on gusty wind, wind sports unaffected by small chop.

title: Strict per-spot tide gate for tide-critical launches
description: Codex #4. The current tide gate bottoms at 0.3 and only reaches it after >2 tide windows — fine for 'better at this tide', too soft for 'unsafe/inaccessible except at this tide'. Add a strict mode driven by the per-spot metadata (sibling bead): for tide-critical spots, cap hard (lower floor and/or faster falloff, optionally hard-fail at clearly-unsafe states) while leaving the soft gate as the default for preference-only spots. Depends on the per-spot safety metadata.
acceptance_criteria: Strict-tide spots cap sharply at the wrong/unsafe state while soft-preference spots keep current behaviour; configurable per spot; tide-gate tests cover both modes.

title: Per-spot safety metadata in config flow
description: Codex #4 + safety enablers. Extend the spot config with optional safety metadata that the gate/flag logic consumes: (1) unsafe tide states + a strict-tide-gate flag (distinct from merely-suboptimal tide preference) for inaccessible/dangerous-except-at-tide launches; (2) an 'offshore wind unsafe' toggle (default on for SUP/sea-swim/beginner craft) so the offshore-wind safety flag fires; (3) optional launch-type. Offshore wind-direction sectors already partly exist (wind_dirs). Feeds the strict tide gate (sibling) and the offshore-wind safety flag.

title: Provider-authority map + 'better source available' recommendations
description: Encode per-domain (and where relevant per-region) provider authority: e.g. UKHO gold for UK tides; keyed marine models > Open-Meteo for swell at exposed spots; Open-Meteo fine for wind. Compare each spot's actual routing (al8.4) to the authority ranking and proactively flag where a better source is available — via HA Repairs and/or a diagnostic sensor, not noise on every entity.
acceptance_criteria: A spot routing swell to a weaker-than-available source raises an actionable 'better source available' nudge naming the domain + suggested source; no nudge when already on the best available.
close_reason: Provider-authority map shipped: authority.py ranks providers per domain (UKHO gold for UK tides region-gated; keyed marine > open_meteo for exposed-coast swell; wind has no hierarchy). recommend_sources() compares actual al8.1/al8.4 routing vs best AVAILABLE source -> nudge only when a strictly better configured source is unused. Surfaced via a per-spot DIAGNOSTIC 'Source advice' sensor + get_overview source_advice (no per-entity noise).

title: Cross-provider confidence (ensemble spread) — supersedes al8.5
description: Fetch >=2 sources for a domain and compute agreement -> confidence; offer an optional consensus blend for accuracy. One multi-source fetch yields both the spread (confidence) and the blend. Budget-aware via the al8.2 overlay machinery + free-tier throttle. This is al8.5 (ensemble) reframed as confidence-first.
design: Reuse _overlay_forecast TTL cache; align by timestamp (overlay.merge already does); per-field: confidence = f(spread), blended = weighted mean (opt-in). Gated on cost — only for spots/domains worth the extra calls.
acceptance_criteria: A spot configured with 2 marine sources shows a confidence from their agreement; large divergence reads as low confidence; optional blend opt-in; stays within free-tier budget.
close_reason: Cross-provider ensemble shipped: ensemble_marine() derives per-field confidence from base-vs-overlay agreement (reuses al8.2 overlay TTL/free-tier throttle); optional consensus blend (CONF_MARINE_BLEND) with circular mean for directions. Opt-in via CONF_MARINE_ENSEMBLE. Confidence flows to existing sensor/get_overview surfaces via source_confidence.

title: Per-domain source routing: CompositeProvider + per-spot config + UI

Fetch each source once, merge field-by-field by priority into one SpotForecast. Single-provider is the trivial config. Expose per-spot source selection in add/edit-spot UI (currently spot['provider'] is read but never set by the UI).
design: CompositeProvider wraps N ForecastProviders + tide overlay; each domain pulled from its assigned source; budget-aware scheduling per source. Config stored per spot (routing map) with sensible defaults (all Open-Meteo).
acceptance_criteria: A spot can route wind=open_meteo, waves=stormglass, tide=ukho via the UI; merged result scored; defaults unchanged for existing spots.
close_reason: Per-spot per-domain source routing: resolve_route() + per-spot provider/marine/tide overrides in edit-spot (inherit global). No CompositeProvider class — coordinator pipeline is the composite. Module-ordering bug caught by HA guard + fixed; guard hardened to render edit_spot_fields. Merged PR #9.

title: Optional WorldTides keyed global tide overlay
description: Add WorldTides as an OPTIONAL, user-supplies-key global tide provider behind the region-resolver, for spots outside UK/US that want harmonic-grade tides instead of the Open-Meteo modeled fallback. WorldTides: ~8000+ stations global, harmonic+gauge blend, prepaid-credit (small free allowance then paid) so MUST be user-keyed (never bundle a shared key). Lower priority — the Open-Meteo fallback already gives global zero-config coverage; this is a precision upgrade. Confirm WorldTides terms/pricing live before building (earlier figures came from search snippets).

title: Ensemble / consensus blending for accuracy (optional)
description: When multiple sources supply the SAME field, blend (average/weight) for model-consensus accuracy and de-biasing. Only worth it where a single source is systematically biased at our spots and the extra calls are justified. Lowest priority; gated on routing landing and evidence of bias.
acceptance_criteria: Configurable per-field blend of >=2 sources with weights; provenance records the blend; opt-in only.

value: Sport profiles in sports.py calibrated against real Open-Meteo for the Christchurch spot set (June 2026): one light-SE day (validate_spots.py) + 5 windiest of trailing 21 days (analyze_history.py, gusts to 44kn). Re-run both after any profile change. UPDATE (al8.3): surf now consumes swell QUALITY — _swell_factor scores swell period (long-period groundswell > short-period windswell; surf swell_period_ideal_s=11, weight_swell=0.7) × swell direction (per-spot swell_dirs window, gated only when provider gives swell_dir_deg). Offshore WIND direction already scored via _dir_factor + per-spot wind_dirs (surf weight_dir=0.8). swell_dir_deg now fetched by open_meteo (swell_wave_direction), stormglass (swellDirection), windy (swell1_direction). Re-calibrated post-change: wind sports unaffected, surf marginal on flat 3s windswell (correct), 'too strong' on onshore gales. Prior 'surf scored on wind alone' residual is RESOLVED. wingfoil/kitesurf wave+gust ceilings raised to stop strong-day hard-fails.

Safety is a SEPARATE first-class output from the suitability score (epic swelligence-slh, from a Codex review 2026-06-24): score=how good the session looks, confidence=how trustworthy the forecast is, safety_flags=why it may be unsafe. Flags may optionally cap score but ONLY where sport-relevant (offshore wind is good for surf, dangerous for SUP/sea-swim/beginner-wing/inflatables). HONESTY RULES (non-negotiable): rip risk is never directly observed — infer from surf size+swell period+tide+current+wind/wave and label 'elevated rip risk', NEVER 'rip present'; currents (current_speed_kn/dir) are MODEL hints, not beach-specific certainty. RESTRAINT: over-flagging destroys trust — thresholds conservative, every flag individually justified, benign conditions must produce zero flags (assert in tests). Foundation is factor-completeness semantics: distinguish not_applicable (sport doesn't score it; free) vs not_configured (spot metadata missing; surface as nudge, don't silently relax strictness) vs missing_provider_data (essential field None; cap/penalize) — the current score_point drops None factors from the denominator, which inflates scores and advantages untuned spots. Existing wind/wave hard-fails should be UNIFIED into the safety-flag model (too strong/too big/too choppy as explicit flags), not a parallel mechanism.

Stormglass provider live-verified 2026-06-23 against the real API (Avon Beach): weather (m/s->kn, sg-source pick, C temps, marine) + 12 tide events normalise correctly. Free plan = 10 requests/day; async_fetch costs 2 (weather + tide/extremes). At default 30-min polling one spot would use ~96/day and exhaust the quota in ~1.5h. The 'Free tier' checkbox on the providers options step sets CONF_PROVIDERS[key].free_tier; integration then caps poll interval via free_tier_min_interval_minutes() = max(user, safe), safe=360min for 1 Stormglass spot (80% safety, budget/2 per fetch, /n_spots). Windy + UKHO normalisers still unit-test-only (no keys).

GitHub PAT for f00lycooly (classic ghp_) for the swelligence GitHub mirror: Vault knowledge/swelligence/dev/github-pat (fields token/username/email/purpose/source). Readable by the swelligence-dev AppRole (policy swelligence-dev grants RW on knowledge/swelligence/dev/*, RO on prod). Creds in ~/.vault-creds/swelligence-dev-{role-id,secret-id} (token_ttl 1h, secret_id no expiry). Auth: source ~/.claude/scripts/vault-auth.sh swelligence  (or admin token ~/.vault-creds/admin-token). Same PAT authenticates the Forgejo->GitHub push mirror AND github.com API ops (authed=5000/hr vs anon 60/hr). Original copy also at knowledge/ObsidianBrain/dev/github-pat.

ARCHITECTURE RULE (user directive 2026-06-25): the HA integration computes; the screens (HA Lovelace card AND the LVGL wall panel) are THIN RENDERERS that do as little processing as possible. Any semantic computation — suitability scoring, the daytime-only daily/weekly outlook, tide state (rising/falling + next high/low), the hourly series, best window — must be produced by the integration as ready-to-render DATA POINTS (entity attributes or the swelligence.get_forecast service), not derived in card/panel JS or in screen-side prep. Existing integration builders already fit: forecast.hourly_forecast (padded-daylight hourly slots), forecast.daily_forecast (one entry/day = that day's PEAK among daylight hours = the daytime-only weekly metric), tide.tide_state (added: rising/falling + next high/low, prefers real tide_events overlay, falls back to modelled sea_level_m). Mockup pipeline mirrors this: scripts/sample_spot.py (= integration provider+scorer) emits daily+tide into forecast.json; scripts/build_card_data.py only formats (vcode packing, dow labels, near-term slicing) and embeds; the HTML/JS only renders. Productionization (expose tide_state + outlook as live attributes/service for the real card+panel) tracked in a bead.
